app_name.doc: App name shown in browser tab + nav header.

loading.doc: Generic placeholder shown while a list is fetching.

busy.doc: Spinner label shown while a skill call is running.

error.doc: Generic error prefix; renders before the API message body.

empty.doc: Generic empty-state copy for any list with zero items.

inbox.doc: Top-nav link to /inbox.html. Shows a count badge when notifications are unread.

ready.doc: Status pill on a task with no open dependencies, no assignee yet.

waiting.doc: Status pill on a task whose dependencies are not all complete.

blocked.doc: Status pill on a task that cannot proceed (e.g. removed dep).

claimed.doc: Status pill on a task with an assignee, no submission yet.

submitted.doc: Status pill on a task whose assignee has marked it submitted; awaiting approver.

rejected.doc: Status pill on a task whose approver pushed it back. Assignee can re-submit.

complete.doc: Status pill on a closed task.

claim.doc: Action button: claim an unassigned task as my own.

release.doc: Action button: assignee gives the task back to the open pool.

complete.doc: Action button: assignee marks a self-mark task done in one step.

submit.doc: Action button: assignee submits a task with an approver-required mode.

approve.doc: Action button: approver signs off on a submitted task.

reject.doc: Action button: approver pushes a submission back; opens a reason prompt.

revoke.doc: Action button: master yanks an assignment back; opens a reason prompt.

appeal.doc: Action button (in inbox): opens a chat thread with the master to dispute a revoke.

reassign.doc: Action button (admin/coord): change the assignee of a task.

remove.doc: Action button (admin only): hard-delete a task.

add_subtask.doc: Action button: spawn a child task under this one.

add_task.doc: Submit button on the add-task composer form.

clear_all.doc: Inbox action: delete every notification (with confirm).

title.doc: Section heading on /index.html for the composer form.

title_label.doc: Form field label for task title.

dod_placeholder.doc: Placeholder shown inside the DoD textarea.

approval_label.doc: Form field label for approval-mode picker.

approval_self_mark.doc: Picker option: assignee finishes without external sign-off.

approval_creator.doc: Picker option: the addedBy/master must approve a submit.

approval_webid.doc: Picker option: a third party named by webid approves.

approver_label.doc: Form field label for the designated-approver webid input.

due_label.doc: Form field label for the dueAt date picker.

result_added.doc: Confirmation shown after a successful addTask. {{id}} is the new task's ULID.

settings_heading.doc: Sub-section heading on /crew.html for the config dump.

stats_heading.doc: Sub-section heading on /crew.html for the metrics tables.

cadences_heading.doc: Admin-only sub-section on /crew.html for editing event cadences.

requests_heading.doc: Admin/coord sub-section on /crew.html for the queue.

paused_banner.text: This crew is paused. New tasks are blocked; existing tasks remain claimable.
paused_banner.doc: Banner shown on every page when crew.paused = true.

archived_banner.text: This crew is archived. The ledger is read-only.
archived_banner.doc: Banner shown on every page when crew.archived = true.

empty_state.doc: Empty-state copy when the inbox has zero entries.

event_missed.doc: Inbox event chip for missed-deadline notifications.

event_completed.doc: Inbox event chip for task-completed notifications.

event_review_needed.doc: Inbox event chip for task-submitted notifications targeting an approver.

event_rejected.doc: Inbox event chip for task-rejected notifications targeting the assignee.

event_revoked.doc: Inbox event chip for task-revoked notifications targeting the previous assignee.

event_subtask.doc: Inbox event chip for subtask-request notifications targeting admins.

intro.text: A crew is a shared task ledger — a household, a project, a team, a friend group, or a neighbourhood-maintenance crew. Three ways to get started:

cta_create.doc: Primary CTA on /welcome.html. Toggles the wizard.

cta_join.text: Join with invite (paste link — coming soon)
cta_join.doc: Secondary CTA. Disabled placeholder until the invite redemption flow lands.

cta_restore.doc: Tertiary CTA. Disabled placeholder until the mnemonic restore flow lands.

wizard_intro.text: You become admin of the crew you create. Invite the rest after.

q3_displayname.doc: Wizard step 3 — local user's display name (optional).

kind_maintenance.doc: Crew kind option (Stoop-adjacent — used when a buurt sets up its own task ledger).

q5_storage.text: 5. Where does the crew's state live?
q5_storage.doc: Wizard step 5 — storage-policy fieldset legend. Mirrors Stoop V2 web's A3 picker.

q5_storage_intro.text: Default keeps the crew on your devices (no Solid pod needed). You can upgrade later. Once pod-having → no way back.
q5_storage_intro.doc: Hint under the storage-policy legend. Contains <strong>.

storage_no_pod_label.doc: Bold label for the no-pod storage radio.

storage_no_pod_hint.text: No pod required. Items replicate across all members' pseudo-pods.

storage_centralised_label.doc: Bold label for the centralised storage radio.

storage_centralised_hint.text: One pod as source of truth. Best for smaller crews.

storage_decentralised_label.text: Each member on their own pod (cross-pod refs).
storage_decentralised_label.doc: Bold label for the decentralised storage radio.

storage_decentralised_hint.text: No central point. Best for large crews.

storage_hybrid_label.text: Hybrid — ledger on shared pod, drafts local.
storage_hybrid_label.doc: Bold label for the hybrid storage radio.

storage_hybrid_hint.text: For crews with occasionally-active members who still want persistence.

storage_group_pod_label.text: Pod URI of the shared group pod
storage_group_pod_label.doc: Label of the optional groupPodUri input (centralised / hybrid only).

result_heading.doc: Heading of the success card on /welcome.html.

result_intro.text: Your crew config has been persisted. Restart Tasks with the new crewId bound at boot (Tasks-v0 binds to one crew per process; multi-crew runtime is a follow-up).
result_intro.doc: Lead text under the success card.

result_footer.text: See /crews.html for the full crew list.
result_footer.doc: Footer hint under the success card. Contains an inline link.

intro.text: Paste the invite payload you got from a crew admin. The invite is a single-use token that proves you were vouched for and carries the role the admin assigned.

invite_hint.text: Paste either the raw JSON or a stoop-invite:// link — both are accepted.

display_label.doc: Label of the optional display-name input.

webid_label.doc: Label of the optional WebID input.

webid_hint.text: Leave blank to derive an opaque pubKey-based identifier.

submit.doc: Submit CTA on the invite form.

result_intro.text: You're now a member of the crew. The proof below is your membership certificate; keep it as a record. The crew agent has been notified of your membership.
result_intro.doc: Lead paragraph under the success heading.

result_footer.text: Open Workspace to see the crew's tasks.
result_footer.doc: Footer hint under the success card.

intro.text: Where the active crew's state lives, and how this device is attached to it. The full state-mapping editor lives in the Hub-web-console (post-P5).

storage_loading.doc: Initial placeholder under the storage heading.

storage_upgrade_oneway.text: Once pod-having → no way back to no-pod. Substrate handles config rewrite only; data migration is your job.
storage_upgrade_oneway.doc: Note shown above the upgrade row when the crew is no-pod (and the upgrade option is visible).

policy_centralised_short.doc: Short label of the centralised option in the upgrade select.

policy_decentralised_short.doc: Short label of the decentralised option in the upgrade select.

policy_hybrid_short.doc: Short label of the hybrid option in the upgrade select.

pod_uri_label.doc: Label of the groupPodUri input on the upgrade row.

signin_intro.text: Pod sign-in lets the crew's state mirror through to your own pod for stronger persistence. Pick an issuer (the Solid provider that holds your pod) and authorize the redirect.
signin_intro.doc: Lead paragraph under the pod-sign-in heading.

signin_issuer_label.doc: Label of the issuer URL input on /pod-settings.html. Default value points at login.inrupt.com.

signout_btn.doc: CTA shown when already signed in; calls signOutOfPod.

registry_heading.doc: H3 of the agent-registry status card.

registry_intro.text: This device is registered in the crew's local agent registry under pseudo-pod://<deviceId>/private/agent-registry. Other agents can look up this device by pubKey, deviceId, or agentUri.
registry_intro.doc: Lead paragraph under the agent-registry heading. Contains an inline <code> example URI.

intro.text: One row per crew you belong to. Counters refresh as tasks change.
intro.doc: Intro paragraph above the dashboard table.

col_overdue.doc: Counter column header — open tasks past their dueAt.

col_review.doc: Counter column header — submitted tasks awaiting my approval.

col_mine.doc: Counter column header — tasks assigned to me.

col_action.doc: Header above the 'Jump in' button column. Single space — the column header is intentionally blank but the locale convention requires non-empty text.

button_jump_in.doc: Button per crew row. Opens the per-crew Workspace in a new tab.

empty_state.text: You don't belong to any crews yet.

saved_heading.doc: H2 of the second table — saved CrewConfigs that aren't bound to the running process. Multi-crew runtime is a follow-up.

saved_intro.text: Crews you provisioned via /welcome.html but aren't currently bound to the running process. Multi-crew runtime is a follow-up; for now you can switch by restarting with the right crew at boot.
saved_intro.doc: Lead paragraph above the saved-crews table. Notes the V2.x deferral. Contains an inline link.

section_heading.doc: Section heading on /mine.html for the planner panel.

section_intro.text: Suggestions only — every slot needs a click. Click `Tweak` to pick a different time.
section_intro.doc: Intro paragraph above the suggestion cards on /mine.html.

button_suggest.doc: Button on /mine.html that calls suggestSchedule for the next 7 days.

button_tweak.doc: Per-suggestion tweak button. Opens the slot picker.

reason_overdue.doc: Reason chip on suggestions for tasks past their dueAt.

reason_last.doc: Reason chip when slack is less than estimateMinutes.

reason_fits.doc: Reason chip when there's plenty of slack.

reason_noslot.doc: Reason chip when no slot fits before the deadline. The user reassigns or extends.

empty_state.text: No suggestions — nothing in your queue with a deadline.
empty_state.doc: Empty-state copy when suggestSchedule returns an empty list.

heading.doc: H2 on /availability.html. Names the surface the user sees.

intro.text: Tap a half-day cell to rotate through `open` / `tight` / `unavailable` / `unknown`. Coordinators see your chips when picking who to assign a task to.
intro.doc: Intro paragraph on /availability.html. Explains what the chips do + who sees them.

opt_in_label.text: Share my availability with crew coordinators
opt_in_label.doc: Checkbox label for the per-member opt-in flag.

opt_in_off.text: Hints are off — your week stays private.
opt_in_off.doc: Status copy when the member is opted out.

disabled_state.text: Availability hints are turned off for this crew. An admin can enable them on the Crew page.
disabled_state.doc: Empty-state copy when crew.availabilityHints.enabled is false.

state_open.doc: Chip label for `open` state — member has flexibility.

state_tight.doc: Chip label for `tight` state — member is busy but can take a small task.

state_unavailable.doc: Chip label for `unavailable` state — member is fully booked.

state_unknown.doc: Chip label for `unknown` state — no hint set.

section_heading_admin.doc: Section heading on /crew.html for the admin-only enable toggle.

section_intro_admin.text: When enabled, members can opt in to share a coarse-grained availability chip per half-day. Coordinators see the chips when picking assignees.
section_intro_admin.doc: Intro paragraph on the /crew.html admin panel.

toggle_label_admin.text: Enable availability hints for this crew
toggle_label_admin.doc: Checkbox label for the admin-only enable flag.

section_heading.doc: Section heading on /crew.html for the V2.2 invoicing panel. Visible only to admin + paid-pro members.

section_intro.text: Compensated members get one invoice line per completed task. Per-month totals here are informational — your actual invoicing flow lives outside Tasks.
section_intro.doc: Intro paragraph above the per-pro table on /crew.html.

toggle_label.doc: Checkbox label for the per-crew compensation.enabled flag (admin only).

rate_label.doc: Column header for the per-member rate (admin-editable).

rate_unit.doc: Suffix on the rate column showing it's per hour. Currency comes from crew.compensation.currency.

month_label.doc: Label for the month selector on the per-pro panel.

totals_label.doc: Label above the count + hours + amount summary row.

informational.doc: Footnote under the totals row. Reminds the user that Tasks is not a billing app.

no_lines.text: No completed tasks yet this month.
no_lines.doc: Empty-state copy when a paid-pro has zero invoice lines for the selected month.

compensated_chip.doc: Chip shown next to a member's name when compensated: true.

section_heading.doc: Section heading on /crew.html for the V2.1 calendar-emission settings panel.

section_intro.text: When on, each member's pod gets a `.ics` file containing their assigned + mastered tasks. Members subscribe once in their phone calendar app; new tasks show up automatically.
section_intro.doc: Intro paragraph above the toggle on /crew.html. Explains the data flow + privacy in plain language.

toggle_label.doc: Checkbox label for the per-crew calendarEmission.enabled flag (admin/coord only).

toggle_admin_only.text: Only admins or coordinators can change this.
toggle_admin_only.doc: Disabled-state hint shown when the toggle is read-only for the current actor.

url_label.doc: Label above the per-member URL the calendar app subscribes to.

url_copy_hint.text: Copy + paste into your phone calendar app's 'Subscribe' / 'Add by URL' option.
url_copy_hint.doc: Hint shown below the URL input on /crew.html.

off_state.text: Calendar sync is off. An admin can turn it on above.
off_state.doc: Empty-state copy when calendarEmission.enabled is false.

write_failed.text: Couldn't write the calendar file. (Pod offline or read-only.)
write_failed.doc: Error toast when wireCalendarEmission's write throws. Translates the substrate's storage error code.

tooltip_open_deps.doc: Tooltip on the disabled 'Mark complete' / 'Approve' button when V2.7's hard-dependency gate would block the close. {{count}} = number of open deps; {{ids}} = comma-separated short-id list.

button_force.doc: Admin-only button on a task with `computeStatus === 'waiting'`. Opens the reason-prompt dialog.

force_reason_prompt.text: Reason for force-completing (mandatory; recorded in audit log):
force_reason_prompt.doc: Label above the textarea in the force-complete confirm dialog.

force_audit_label.doc: Audit-log action label for force-complete entries. Translators: keep it short — fits in a chip.

bot_cannot_close.doc: Bot reply when bot.markComplete or bot.approve fires the V2.7 dependency gate.

card_title.doc: Inbox-card title on a subtask-proposal item targeting the current member.

card_intro.text: Approving this rolls your submission back to claimed. Original submit stays in the task's history.
card_intro.doc: Small text under the card title explaining the consequence of approval.

approve_button.doc: Approve button on the proposal card.

decline_button.doc: Decline button on the proposal card.

propose_mode_label.doc: Button label on the master/coord side when the parent is submitted; replaces the normal 'Add sub-task' label.

propose_mode_intro.text: Adding scope after submission needs the assignee's consent. They'll see this proposal in their inbox.
propose_mode_intro.doc: Hint shown next to the propose-sub-task button.

permission_denied.text: You do not have permission to do that.

already_claimed.doc: Friendly claim-race message. {{actor}} is the winner's webid.

missing_reason.doc: Surfaced when revoke/reject is called without the mandatory reason/note.

missing_note.doc: Surfaced when reject is called without the mandatory note.

crew_paused.text: This crew is paused. Adding new tasks is blocked.
crew_paused.doc: Surfaced when addTask is called while crew.paused = true.

crew_archived.text: This crew is archived. The ledger is read-only.
crew_archived.doc: Surfaced when addTask or any mutation is called while crew.archived = true.
